definition: Lamentation or outward grieving expression associated with suffering.
notes: The project prefers lamentation because it fits standard formula passages and preserves the public, expressive dimension of the term. Wailing can work in more vivid narrative contexts, but it is too narrow as a default.

context_rules.context: In most doctrinal and formulaic contexts
In more vivid or narrative settings
context_rules.notes: Acceptable when the expressive force is foregrounded.

example_phrases.translation: sorrow, lamentation, dissatisfaction, distress, and despair

example_phrases.notes: Useful verbal expression of the noun's force.
